originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and earth_sun scripts, then initializes many inline orbit, daylight, and seasonal sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, route-local textures, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, globe textures, and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the drag, date, time, and seasonal scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a draggable globe sunlight scene and continues through ellipse, orbit, and Kepler-law sections driven by date or progress sliders and synchronized canvas updates.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening globe drag scene, date and time sliders, and the early orbit and Kepler sections under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one opening globe drag path, one date or time slider path, and one orbital scene as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from ellipse geometry into orbital motion instead of flattening the early sections into static diagrams.
evidence: Post-gps rollout planning explicitly called for one globe drag, one date or time slider, and one orbital-plane or seasonal scene.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening Earth sunlight scene responds to drag, a time slider updates the rendered state, and a later tropical-year scene remains interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through sidereal versus solar day, axial tilt, cosine-lighting, analemma, precession, and tropical-year sections with linked canvases and slider-controlled date progression.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later day-length, seasonal, and year-tracking sections intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The published globe scenes now load their texture assets from the route-local Earth and Sun image tree instead of the upstream root path.
Newly satisfiable cross-links from later Bartosz routes can now point locally to the ellipse and sidereal-day sections.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one later seasonal or tropical-year scene as parity-critical for the post-gps batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a later tropical-year or equivalent seasonal scene updates from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with further-reading sections and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance, while later Bartosz articles refer back to Earth and Sun sections by upstream URL.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published closing sections while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and enabling later local intra-family links to target this route.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The `gps` route now rewrites its ellipse and sidereal-day references to the local Earth and Sun route.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit now flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Route cleanup updated existing Bartosz cross-links so the family converges toward local intra-family navigation as new pages land.
